title: Welinq and Pasqal Accelerate Networked Quantum Computing with Neutral-Atom Technology - Pasqal
why:
- Welinq and Pasqal are accelerating the development of networked quantum computing using interconnected neutral-atom quantum processors.
- Their collaboration is moving from standalone quantum processing units toward quantum architectures that can be deployed in data centers and scaled by networking multiple quantum processors.
- The partnership has reached a new milestone with the InterQo project, a €4 million initiative supported by the Île-de-France Region and BPI France through the France 2030 program.
- Pasqal and Welinq both develop neutral-atom technologies that are natively compatible for both quantum computing and quantum networking, enabling a coherent approach to building quantum computer clusters.
- Welinq has developed a high-rate entanglement generation platform (“quantum Ethernet port”) and demonstrated the most powerful neutral atom–based quantum memory to date, moving laboratory technology into deployable components for data centers.
- The InterQo project focuses on designing quantum processors with built-in networking capabilities and integrating efficient photon extraction systems to connect them, providing crucial steps toward large-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computing.
